about.title: About Us
about.intro: Express web is an agency initiative within the GBS platform, dedicated to providing fast, modern, and affordable online presence for small and medium-sized businesses.
about.p1: GBS (Global Business System) is a technology platform that unifies multiple independent services and applications under a modular umbrella. Express web is designed as a starting point – a practical tool for quick website creation.
about.p2: Express packages allow users to get a basic presentation in just 10 minutes, a PRO version within a few hours, and full-featured e-commerce or business applications in the following stages.
about.p3: Our team combines experience in programming (Laravel, Vue, Tailwind), design, UI/UX practices, and business process and sales system development. Solutions are locally made – developed in Serbia, with an understanding of the local market.
about.p4: The platform is scalable: a user can start with a free website and later upgrade their solution – without migration or switching systems. Everything is already ready – you just unlock additional modules as needed.
about.p5: Express web is not the end – it is an entry point. Through the GBS platform, we develop new services: ERP, CRM, marketplaces, accounting apps, educational tools, travel agency systems, and more.
about.p6: Our goal is to make digital transformation not expensive, complicated, or reserved for big players – but available to everyone, immediately and tailored to their needs.

lead.first: A business website is not merely a digital business card. It is often the first place where a potential client discovers your company, evaluates whether you appear trustworthy and decides whether to contact you.
lead.second: That is why choosing a website should not begin with the question of which colour you like or which template looks best. You should first understand the business goal, user needs and the way the website will support the company's development.

meta.title: How to choose a business website that truly delivers results
meta.excerpt: A business website is more than a digital business card. A well-designed website presents the company, builds trust and helps turn visitors into clients.
meta.seoTitle: How to choose a business website | Express Web
meta.seoDescription: Learn what a quality business website should include and how to choose a solution that supports the growth of your business.

goal.title: Start with the business goal
goal.first: Before discussing design and technology, define what you expect visitors to do when they arrive on your website.
goal.second: Different business goals require different website structures, content and calls to action.

inquiries.title: Receiving inquiries
inquiries.description: The website should clearly present your services and allow visitors to quickly send an inquiry or schedule a conversation.

sales.title: Selling products
sales.description: You need a clear catalogue, a simple purchasing process and reliable information about payment and delivery.

trust.title: Building trust
trust.description: References, projects, reviews and company information help users make a more confident decision.

information.title: Informing users
information.description: The website can serve as a knowledge centre with guides, frequently asked questions and expert content.

warning.title: A common mistake
warning.text: Many companies choose the design first and only later think about content and objectives. The result may be a visually attractive website that does not clearly explain the offer or deliver measurable business results.

audience.title: Think from your client's perspective
audience.first: A business owner knows their products and services well, but a website visitor may be hearing about the company for the first time. They will not have the same knowledge, patience or context.
audience.second: That is why the content should be clear and easy to understand. Visitors must quickly understand what you do, who you help and what the next step is.
audience.third: A good website does not only talk about the company. It answers users' questions, resolves their concerns and guides them towards a decision.

essentials.title: What a modern business website should include
essentials.intro: Regardless of the industry, several elements form the foundation of a quality business website.

message.title: A clear primary message
message.description: In the first section of the page, visitors should immediately understand what you offer and who the offer is intended for.

mobile.title: Mobile responsiveness
mobile.description: The website must be clear and easy to use on phones, tablets and computers.

speed.title: Fast loading
speed.description: Slow pages increase the likelihood that visitors will leave before learning about your offer.

contact.title: Easy contact
contact.description: A phone number, email address, contact form and clear call to action should be easy to find.

seo.title: SEO foundation
seo.description: Titles, descriptions, content structure and technical settings help search engines understand the website.

security.title: Security and maintenance
security.description: An SSL certificate, regular updates and backups protect the website and user data.

callout.title: The website should provide a clear next step
callout.text: Every important page should show users what they can do next: send an inquiry, call, schedule a conversation, view projects or purchase a product.

design.title: Design should support the content
design.first: Professional design is important because it influences the first impression and trust. However, its purpose is not to overshadow information, but to make it clearer and easier to understand.
design.second: Too many animations, small text, complicated navigation and decorative elements can make the website more difficult to use.
design.third: The best design reflects the company's identity, clearly highlights the most important information and enables users to move easily through the content.

price.title: Do not choose a website based only on the lowest price
price.first: Price is important, especially for a small company or a new business. However, the cheapest solution is not always the most cost-effective in the long term.
price.second: You should check what the offer includes: content preparation, mobile version, domain, hosting, security, SEO settings, support and the possibility of future upgrades.
price.third: A well-planned website can grow together with the company. A poorly established foundation often leads to additional costs and a complete redesign.

steps.title: Five steps when choosing a business website
steps.intro: A simple process can help you avoid the wrong investment and obtain a solution that meets your actual needs.

goal.title: Define the goal
goal.description: Determine whether you want more inquiries, sales, bookings, presentation of services or a combination of several objectives.

content.title: Prepare the key information
content.description: Collect information about the company, services, products, contact details, references and frequently asked questions.

scope.title: Define the initial scope
scope.description: You do not need to build everything immediately. The first version should be high quality and ready for future expansion.

partner.title: Choose a reliable partner
partner.description: Look for clear communication, a realistic plan, a transparent offer and support after the website is launched.

growth.title: Plan for growth
growth.description: Consider a future blog, online sales, multilingual content, integrations and other functionalities.

recommendation.title: Express Web recommendation
recommendation.text: Start with a solution that meets today's needs, but build it on a foundation that supports future growth. You do not need a large system immediately, but it is important to avoid an architecture that will limit you later.

conclusion.title: A good business website begins with a good decision
conclusion.first: A quality website connects business goals, user needs, content, design and technology into one functional whole.
conclusion.second: When it is properly planned, a website does more than present a company. It builds trust, supports sales, improves communication and becomes an important part of business development.

goals.caption: The website structure should be adapted to specific business objectives.

checklist.caption: A clear message, mobile responsiveness, speed, SEO, easy contact and security form the foundation of a modern website.

meta.title: When is the right time to launch an online store?
meta.excerpt: Many entrepreneurs want an online store as soon as possible, but the real question is not when you can build one, but when it will genuinely contribute to the growth of your business.
meta.seoTitle: When to launch an online store | Express Web
meta.seoDescription: Learn how to assess whether your business is ready for an online store and what you need to prepare before starting online sales.

lead.first: Online stores are no longer reserved for large companies. Small businesses, family-run companies and agricultural holdings can also successfully sell products online.
lead.second: However, the fact that it is possible to launch an online store does not mean that it is always the right next step. In many cases, a quality presentation website or product catalogue can deliver better results than a poorly prepared online store.

need.title: Do you really need an online store?
need.first: Not every business sells in the same way. Some companies only need a presentation website, others need a product catalogue, while some require a complete online sales system.
need.second: If customers need to speak with an expert, receive an individual offer or define their requirements precisely before purchasing, a traditional online store may not be the best first step.
need.third: An online store makes the most sense when products can be presented clearly, prices are known and the ordering, payment and delivery process can be standardised.

readiness.title: How to know whether you are ready for online sales
readiness.intro: Before developing a store, check whether the basic business and organisational conditions are in place.

products.title: Your products are ready for sale
products.description: The offer is defined, products are available and stock levels can be monitored and updated regularly.

catalog.title: You can maintain the catalogue
catalog.description: Someone is available to add products, change prices, update availability and verify information.

delivery.title: Delivery is organised
delivery.description: You know who delivers the goods, what the costs are, which areas you cover and how long delivery takes.

support.title: You can support customers
support.description: Someone is responsible for questions, orders, complaints, exchanges and other forms of customer support.

marketing.title: You are ready to promote the store
marketing.description: There is a plan for how customers will discover the store through Google, social media, content or other channels.

warning.title: The most common mistake
warning.text: An online store does not automatically increase sales. It is only one part of a system that must connect products, content, marketing, logistics, payments and customer support.

preparation.title: What to prepare before you begin
preparation.intro: The better the preparation, the faster the development process and the easier the store will be to manage later.

photos.title: Quality product photos
photos.description: Customers cannot physically inspect the product, so the images should clearly present its appearance, details and intended use.

descriptions.title: Clear product descriptions
descriptions.description: Descriptions should answer the most important questions and help customers determine whether the product meets their needs.

prices.title: Prices and availability
prices.description: Prices must be accurate, while stock and availability information should be updated regularly.

payment.title: Payment methods
payment.description: Define whether you will offer card payments, cash on delivery, bank transfers or a combination of methods.

shipping.title: Shipping and costs
shipping.description: Customers should clearly understand the shipping price, expected delivery time and any free or local delivery conditions.

returns.title: Complaints and returns
returns.description: Clearly explain the procedure for complaints, product exchanges and refunds.

legal.title: Legal information
legal.description: Terms of use, privacy policy and seller details should be available and aligned with your business model.

technology.title: Technology is not the greatest challenge
technology.first: Today, quality platforms allow an online store to be developed relatively quickly.
technology.second: The greater challenge is organising everyday operations: processing orders, updating stock, packing, shipping, communicating with customers and resolving complaints.
technology.third: The technology should therefore match real business processes, the number of products, expected sales volume and future growth plans.

marketing.title: How will customers find your store?
marketing.text: Without traffic, there are no sales. The development of an online store should therefore be connected with SEO, a Google Business Profile, paid campaigns, social media, email marketing and quality content.

marketplace.title: Online store or marketplace?
marketplace.first: If you sell your own products, a traditional online store is usually the simpler and more suitable solution.
marketplace.second: If you want multiple independent sellers to offer products on the same platform, you need a marketplace system with seller management, commissions, orders and platform rules.
marketplace.third: These are two different business models. A marketplace is not merely a larger online store, but a significantly more complex business and technological system.

steps.title: Five steps towards a considered decision
steps.intro: Before development begins, go through a simple assessment process.

assessment.title: Assess customer needs
assessment.description: Determine how customers currently find products, what is missing and whether they want to order online.

scope.title: Define the initial offer
scope.description: You do not need to publish the entire assortment immediately. Start with the products that are most popular and easiest to deliver.

operations.title: Organise the processes
operations.description: Decide who receives orders, updates stock, packs products, communicates with customers and handles complaints.

platform.title: Choose the right platform
platform.description: The solution should match the scale of the business, the budget, required integrations and future development plans.

promotion.title: Prepare a promotional plan
promotion.description: Define the channels, content, budget and performance measurement before the store is launched.

recommendation.title: Express Web recommendation
recommendation.text: Do not launch an online store simply because your competitors have one. Launch it when it can simplify sales, improve the customer experience and deliver measurable business results.

conclusion.title: An online store is a tool, not the goal
conclusion.first: When properly planned, an online store can expand the market, increase sales and allow customers to order whenever it suits them.
conclusion.second: When business processes are not ready, it becomes an additional cost and a source of problems. The best time to launch is therefore when the offer, logistics, support and promotion are sufficiently well organised.

cta.description: We will help you assess your needs and choose the right solution — from a product catalogue to a complete online store or marketplace platform.

preparation.caption: Good preparation of the catalogue and business processes reduces problems after online sales begin.

meta.title: Why is a Google Business Profile not enough for a serious business?
meta.excerpt: A Google Business Profile is an excellent tool for local visibility, but it cannot fully replace a business website, quality content and a long-term SEO strategy.
meta.seoTitle: Why a Google Business Profile is not enough | Express Web
meta.seoDescription: Learn what a Google Business Profile can do for your company, where its limitations are and why it works best together with a business website.

lead.first: A Google Business Profile is one of the most useful free tools available to local businesses. It allows a company to appear in Google Search and Maps, display contact information, opening hours, photos and reviews.
lead.second: The problem begins when a company treats its Google profile as a complete replacement for a business website. The profile can be an important entry point, but it does not provide the control, content space or development options required for a serious digital presence.

profile.title: What is a Google Business Profile?
profile.first: Google Business Profile is a free Google tool designed for companies that want to manage how they appear in local search results and on Google Maps.
profile.second: A profile can include the company name, address, phone number, opening hours, business category, photos, posts, products, services and customer reviews.
profile.third: For local businesses, practices, stores, restaurants, service providers and consultants, a well-maintained profile can be one of the fastest ways to become more visible to potential customers.

benefits.title: What a Google Business Profile does very well
benefits.intro: When properly configured and regularly updated, the profile can significantly improve a company's local visibility.

maps.title: Visibility on Google Maps
maps.description: Users can quickly find the business location, obtain directions and check the distance.

contact.title: Fast access to contact details
contact.description: The phone number, address, opening hours and website link are available directly from search results.

reviews.title: Customer reviews
reviews.description: Positive experiences from existing customers can help new users develop trust.

updates.title: Posts and updates
updates.description: The company can publish offers, news, events and other short updates.

insights.title: Basic customer insights
insights.description: The profile shows how users found the company and which actions they took.

warning.title: A common misconception
warning.text: A strong Google Business Profile can improve visibility, but it does not mean that a company has a complete digital presence. The profile belongs to Google's platform, follows a predefined structure and cannot replace a website built around specific business objectives.

limitations.title: What a Google Business Profile cannot replace
limitations.intro: The profile has a clear purpose, but its limitations become apparent when a company wants to develop a more serious online presence.

control.title: Full control over content
control.description: Google determines the appearance, structure and display rules, while a company controls the entire user experience on its own website.

content.title: Detailed pages and expert content
content.description: A profile is not suitable for presenting services, projects, guides, case studies and answers to complex questions in depth.

brand.title: A distinctive brand identity
brand.description: Colours, typography, layout, messaging and visual identity are limited by Google's standard presentation.

sales.title: Advanced sales functionality
sales.description: Contact forms, bookings, calculators, online sales, integrations and automation require a dedicated system.

dependency.title: Independence from a third-party platform
dependency.description: Rules, features and display methods can change, while a business website is a digital asset controlled by the company.

comparison.title: Google Business Profile or business website?
comparison.first: This should not be treated as a choice between two competing solutions. A Google Business Profile and a business website serve different purposes and work best together.
comparison.second: The profile helps users discover the company in local search, quickly verify basic information and read reviews.
comparison.third: The website then provides space to explain the offer in detail, build the brand, publish content, collect enquiries and introduce new functionality.

strategy.title: What a strong combined strategy looks like
strategy.intro: The best results come when the profile, website and content are connected into one consistent system.

profile.title: Optimise the Google Business Profile
profile.description: Enter accurate information, select relevant categories, add photos and regularly publish updates.

website.title: Connect the profile to a quality website
website.description: The link should lead to a page that clearly presents the company, its services and the next step for the user.

content.title: Publish useful content
content.description: Guides, case studies and answers to common questions build authority and improve organic visibility.

analytics.title: Measure the results
analytics.description: Use Search Console and analytics to understand which searches, pages and content generate visits and enquiries.

reputation.title: Develop your reputation
reputation.description: Request genuine reviews, respond to users and consistently maintain high communication standards.

callout.title: Google should guide users towards your own digital space
callout.text: A Google Business Profile is most useful when it does not keep users on a list of basic details, but directs them to a website where they can learn more about the company and make a decision.

reviews.title: Reviews are important, but they are not enough
reviews.first: Reviews can strongly influence trust, especially when a user encounters a company for the first time.
reviews.second: However, a star rating does not always explain what the company actually does, what the cooperation process looks like or how its offer differs from competitors.
reviews.third: The strongest effect is created when Google reviews support quality content, references, a portfolio and clear information on the business website.

recommendation.title: Express Web recommendation
recommendation.text: Use the Google Business Profile as an important local visibility channel, but build the business website as the central point of your digital presence. The profile attracts users, while the website should inform, persuade and guide them towards the next step.

conclusion.title: The Google profile is the entrance, while the website is your digital home
conclusion.first: A Google Business Profile can help a company get discovered, receive more calls, visits and reviews.
conclusion.second: Long-term authority, brand development, content and sales processes require a dedicated website that works together with the Google profile rather than being replaced by it.

meta.title: How can AI help a small business today?
meta.excerpt: Artificial intelligence is not reserved for large companies. Small businesses can already use it for marketing, sales, administration, data analysis and everyday organisation.
meta.seoTitle: How AI can help a small business | Express Web
meta.seoDescription: Learn how a small business can practically use artificial intelligence for marketing, sales, administration, analytics and saving time.

lead.first: Artificial intelligence is often presented as a technology that will completely transform business, replace people or solve almost every problem. Such claims create equal amounts of excitement and fear.
lead.second: For a small business, it is more useful to ask a simpler question: which everyday tasks can AI make faster, easier or better today?

definition.title: AI is a business assistant, not a magical solution
definition.first: Modern AI tools can analyse text, prepare content drafts, summarise documents, suggest ideas, assist with data processing and automate some repetitive tasks.
definition.second: Their greatest value is not in independently running a company, but in helping owners and employees complete certain tasks faster and spend more time on decisions that require experience and responsibility.
definition.third: The quality of the result depends on clear instructions, good input data and professional review by the person using the tool.

warning.title: The biggest misconception about artificial intelligence
warning.text: AI will not automatically repair poorly organised processes or replace business strategy. If a company lacks clear objectives, responsibilities and quality data, AI may simply produce incorrect or unusable results more quickly.

uses.title: Five areas where AI can help today
uses.intro: A small company does not need to introduce complex systems immediately. The benefit can begin with several practical tasks that are regularly repeated.

marketing.title: Marketing and content
marketing.description: AI can help prepare drafts for posts, headlines, product descriptions, emails, blog articles and campaign ideas.

sales.title: Sales and customer communication
sales.description: It can prepare answers to common questions, proposal drafts, conversation summaries and suggestions for follow-up communication.

administration.title: Administration
administration.description: AI can summarise long documents, prepare meeting notes, draft business emails, organise notes and assist with translation.

analytics.title: Data analysis
analytics.description: It can help interpret spreadsheets, sales results, costs, trends and unusual changes that require further attention.

education.title: Learning and research
education.description: AI can explain concepts, support market research, help prepare presentations and accelerate learning in a new field.

dailyWork.title: What practical use looks like in a small company
dailyWork.first: A store owner can use AI to prepare product descriptions, social media ideas and responses to frequently asked customer questions.
dailyWork.second: A consultant can summarise meeting notes, prepare a proposal draft and organise topics for a professional article.
dailyWork.third: A small manufacturer can analyse sales data, prepare a promotional plan and create multilingual versions of basic content, with mandatory professional review.

callout.title: The best result comes from cooperation between people and AI tools
callout.text: People define the goal, provide context, verify facts and make decisions. AI accelerates preparation, suggests options and assists with processing large amounts of information.

limitations.title: What AI cannot and should not replace
limitations.intro: Responsible use begins with understanding its limitations.

context.title: Deep knowledge of a specific business
context.description: AI does not automatically understand your customers, internal processes, supplier relationships or market specifics.

responsibility.title: Business and legal responsibility
responsibility.description: Decisions, contracts, financial data and professional opinions must remain under the control of a responsible person.

experience.title: Experience and professional judgement
experience.description: A tool can suggest options, but it does not possess real experience gained by working with people and practical problems.

accuracy.title: Accuracy verification
accuracy.description: AI can make mistakes, omit important details or present incorrect information as if it were reliable.

relationships.title: Human relationships and trust
relationships.description: Empathy, negotiation, responsibility and long-term client relationships cannot be reduced to an automated response.

steps.title: How to start using AI tools with limited risk
steps.intro: The best approach is to start small and measure the actual benefit.

task.title: Choose one specific task
task.description: Start with a repetitive task that consumes time, such as drafting emails or summarising notes.

tool.title: Choose the right tool
tool.description: Do not try to solve everything with one platform. Choose according to the task, security requirements and data handling.

instructions.title: Provide clear context and instructions
instructions.description: Explain the objective, target audience, tone, limitations and output format you expect.

review.title: Review and improve the result
review.description: Treat AI output as a draft that must be checked, adapted and approved before use.

measure.title: Measure time saved and quality
measure.description: Evaluate whether the task was completed faster and whether the final result is genuinely better.

security.title: Protect data and privacy
security.first: Confidential contracts, passwords, customer data, trade secrets and other sensitive information should not be entered into public AI tools.
security.second: Before introducing a tool, review its terms of use, data retention practices, user permissions and administrative controls.
security.third: For more serious processes, business accounts, controlled integrations and clearly defined internal rules are often the better choice.

recommendation.title: Express Web recommendation
recommendation.text: Do not introduce AI simply because it is popular. Use it where there is a specific problem, a measurable benefit and a person responsible for controlling the result. Start simply, learn through practice and only then automate more complex processes.

conclusion.title: AI works best when it supports people who already understand their work
conclusion.first: Artificial intelligence can help a small business prepare content faster, organise information more effectively, improve communication and reduce time spent on routine tasks.
conclusion.second: Its real value does not come from removing people from the process, but from enabling them to make better decisions and devote more time to customers, development and business strategy.

meta.title: When does a small business need a business application?
meta.excerpt: Learn when spreadsheets, messages and manual tracking are no longer sufficient and how a business application can bring data, processes and reporting together.
meta.seoTitle: When does a business need an application? | Express Web
meta.seoDescription: Recognise the signs that your company needs a business application and learn how to digitalise customers, sales, documents and reporting.

lead.first: Many small companies operate successfully for years using spreadsheets, emails, paper documents and several disconnected programs. This approach may be entirely sufficient while the workload and number of employees remain limited.
lead.second: Problems begin when information is repeatedly entered, lost or delayed, while the owner finds it increasingly difficult to obtain a reliable overview of customers, sales, inventory, tasks and results.

website.title: A business website and a business application have different roles
website.first: A business website primarily presents a company, its products, services, references and contact information. Its purpose is to inform visitors, build trust and encourage an enquiry or purchase.
website.second: A business application supports employees, associates, sales representatives, agents or customers who perform specific activities through the system. It is used to enter and process data, track workflows, assign tasks and prepare reports.
website.third: A website may serve as the entry point into the business, while an application organises what happens after a customer submits an enquiry, places an order or begins a working relationship.

definition.title: A business application should follow the company's real workflow
definition.text: A good application does not force a company to introduce unnecessary steps. It connects existing processes, removes duplication and allows responsible users to see the information they need at any moment.

excel.title: Excel is useful, but it is not always sufficient
excel.first: Excel is an excellent tool for calculations, smaller records, analysis and quickly organising data. For many small companies, it represents a practical and economical starting point.
excel.second: Difficulties begin when several versions of the same spreadsheet exist, multiple employees change the data or customers, products, documents, statuses and responsible people need to be connected.
excel.third: In such an environment, the risk of errors increases, while producing a single report may require manually collecting information from multiple files and messages.

signs.title: Seven signs that a company needs a business application
signs.intro: The decisive factor is not the size of the company, but the complexity of its processes and the amount of information that must be tracked reliably.

duplicateEntry.title: The same data is entered multiple times
duplicateEntry.description: Customer, product or order data is copied from an email into a spreadsheet, from the spreadsheet into a document and then into another program.

scatteredData.title: Information is scattered across different locations
scatteredData.description: Important data is stored in spreadsheets, messages, paper records, employee phones and different cloud services.

manualTracking.title: Statuses are tracked manually
manualTracking.description: Employees must ask one another whether a proposal was sent, an order processed, goods delivered or an invoice paid.

noOverview.title: There is no unified business overview
noOverview.description: The owner or manager cannot easily see what is complete, what is delayed, who is responsible and which customers need attention.

errors.title: Errors and duplicated work become frequent
errors.description: Two people complete the same task, information differs between documents or important obligations are forgotten.

reporting.title: Reporting requires too much time
reporting.description: Reviewing sales, costs or performance requires manually combining data from several spreadsheets and systems.

growth.title: Business growth makes work more complicated
growth.description: Every new customer, employee, location or sales channel increases the number of manual steps and opportunities for errors.

solution.title: Ready-made software or a custom business application?
solution.first: Ready-made solutions are often the best choice when a company has standard requirements, such as invoicing, customer records, project management or basic inventory operations.
solution.second: A custom application makes sense when the company has a specific workflow, multiple user roles, special calculation rules or a need to connect several existing systems.
solution.third: The best solution is sometimes not to build everything from the beginning, but to combine existing software, a custom module and integrations that remove manual data transfer.

warning.title: Do not digitalise a poor process before analysing it
warning.text: If a business process is unclear, unnecessarily complicated or lacks defined responsibilities, an application may only make it faster, not better. Before development begins, the problem should be understood and the workflow simplified.

modules.title: What a business application can bring together
modules.intro: An application can be developed modularly, according to the company's real needs and priorities.

customers.title: Customers and business contacts
customers.description: A unified record of contacts, communication, proposals, contracts, activities and cooperation history.

sales.title: Sales and orders
sales.description: Tracking enquiries, proposals, orders, fulfilment status, payments and sales team activities.

inventory.title: Products and inventory
inventory.description: Information about products, prices, availability, suppliers, incoming stock, outgoing stock and purchasing requirements.

documents.title: Documents
documents.description: Proposals, invoices, contracts, delivery notes, attachments and other records connected with a customer or business process.

tasks.title: Tasks and responsibilities
tasks.description: Task assignment, deadlines, priorities, statuses, notifications and an overview of employee activities.

reports.title: Reports and indicators
reports.description: An overview of sales, performance, costs, delays, key customers and other information needed for decision-making.

automation.title: The greatest value comes from connecting processes
automation.first: A business application should not merely be a digital replacement for a paper form. Its value increases when one action automatically initiates the next step.
automation.second: For example, an accepted proposal can create an order, open a task for the responsible person, prepare a document and send a notification to the customer.
automation.third: This reduces manual work, accelerates processing and creates a clear record of who completed each action and when.

steps.title: How to begin development with limited risk
steps.intro: The safest approach is gradual development with clear priorities and the involvement of people who will use the system every day.

problem.title: Define a specific problem
problem.description: Do not begin with a list of technologies. Describe where time is lost, where errors occur and which information is missing.

process.title: Map the existing process
process.description: Record who starts the activity, which information is used, what happens next and who is responsible for the following step.

priority.title: Choose the most important module
priority.description: The first version should solve one significant problem rather than immediately attempting to replace every program in the company.

prototype.title: Create a simple prototype
prototype.description: Before full development, verify screen layouts, required data, user roles and workflow logic.

testing.title: Test with real users
testing.description: Employees who perform the work can quickly identify unnecessary steps, missing information and impractical functions.

development.title: Develop the application in phases
development.description: Add new modules, automations and integrations only after the core process works reliably and delivers measurable value.

recommendation.title: Express Web recommendation
recommendation.text: Do not begin a project by requesting a CRM, ERP or large business platform. Begin with the specific problem you want to solve. Only after analysis should you decide whether an existing solution, a new integration or a custom application is required.

conclusion.title: The right application creates clarity, not additional complexity
conclusion.first: A business application becomes justified when existing tools no longer support reliable tracking of information, responsibilities and results.
conclusion.second: A well-designed solution should reduce manual work, connect information and allow a company to grow without a proportional increase in administration and errors.

15.description: Dodatna zaštita, keširanje sadržaja, DNS upravljanje i poboljšanje brzine isporuke sajta.

process.label: Način rada
process.title: Kako realizujemo integraciju
process.description: Integracija nije samo unošenje API ključa. Proveravamo poslovni cilj, bezbednost, ograničenja servisa i način održavanja.

1.title: Analiza potrebe
1.description: Definišemo šta treba povezati, koje podatke razmenjujemo i koji rezultat očekujemo.

2.title: Tehnička provera
2.description: Proveravamo API dokumentaciju, uslove korišćenja, dostupnost servisa i bezbednosne zahteve.

3.title: Implementacija i test
3.description: Povezujemo sisteme, testiramo tokove podataka i ponašanje u realnim scenarijima.

4.title: Praćenje i podrška
4.description: Pratimo rad integracije i po potrebi prilagođavamo je promenama servisa ili poslovanja.

security.label: Bezbednost podataka
security.title: Integracije moraju biti pouzdane, ne samo funkcionalne
security.description: Pristupne podatke, dozvole i tokove informacija organizujemo prema potrebama projekta i mogućnostima povezanog servisa.

items.1: API ključevi i pristupni podaci ne prikazuju se u javnom frontend kodu
items.2: Koristimo HTTPS, odgovarajuće dozvole i validaciju podataka
items.3: Vodimo računa o privatnosti, saglasnostima i pravilima povezanih platformi
items.4: Planiramo rezervni način rada kada spoljni servis nije dostupan

cta.title: Želite da povežete sajt sa postojećim poslovnim alatima?
cta.description: Napišite nam koji servis koristite i šta želite da automatizujete. Proverićemo tehničke mogućnosti i predložiti odgovarajuće rešenje.

service_ai_automation.label: AI i automatizacija
service_ai_automation.title: Pametniji poslovni procesi uz veštačku inteligenciju
service_ai_automation.description: Pomažemo firmama da uvedu AI tamo gde može da skrati rutinski rad, ubrza odgovore korisnicima i pretvori postojeće podatke u korisne informacije.

1.title: Manje rutinskog rada
1.description: Automatizujte ponavljajuće zadatke, obradu upita, pripremu odgovora i razmenu podataka.

2.title: Brži odgovori korisnicima
2.description: Omogućite posetiocima i klijentima da brže dobiju informacije, preporuke i osnovnu podršku.

3.title: Bolje korišćenje podataka
3.description: Sažmite informacije, prepoznajte obrasce i pripremite izveštaje koji olakšavaju poslovne odluke.

solutions.label: Moguća rešenja
solutions.title: AI alati prilagođeni konkretnom poslovnom problemu
solutions.description: Ne uvodimo AI samo zato što je popularan. Prvo definišemo zadatak, podatke i očekivani rezultat, a zatim biramo odgovarajuće rešenje.

1.title: AI chatbot i korisnička podrška
1.description: Chatbot koji odgovara na česta pitanja, usmerava korisnike i prikuplja osnovne informacije pre uključivanja zaposlenog.
1.result: Brži odgovori i manje ponavljajućih upita.

2.title: Automatska obrada upita
2.description: Razvrstavanje poruka, kontakt formi i zahteva prema temi, prioritetu ili vrsti korisnika.
2.result: Bolja organizacija i kraće vreme odgovora.

3.title: Poslovni AI asistent
3.description: Interni pomoćnik koji zaposlenima pomaže da pronađu procedure, dokumente, odgovore i informacije iz poslovne baze znanja.
3.result: Brži pristup znanju i ujednačeniji rad tima.

4.title: Preporuke proizvoda i usluga
4.description: Pomoć kupcima pri izboru proizvoda, poređenju opcija i pronalaženju odgovarajućeg rešenja.
4.result: Jednostavnija kupovina i kvalitetniji prodajni upiti.

5.title: AI pretraga sadržaja
5.description: Napredna pretraga dokumenata, članaka, proizvoda ili internih podataka korišćenjem prirodnog jezika.
5.result: Korisnik brže dolazi do relevantne informacije.

6.title: Sažeci i izveštaji
6.description: Automatska priprema sažetaka, pregleda aktivnosti, prodajnih izveštaja i najvažnijih podataka.
6.result: Manje vremena za pripremu i lakše donošenje odluka.

use_cases.label: Primeri primene
use_cases.title: Gde AI može da donese konkretnu korist
use_cases.description: Rešenje se prilagođava delatnosti, dostupnim podacima i načinu na koji firma već posluje.

1.title: Uslužne firme
1.description: Odgovori na česta pitanja, kvalifikovanje upita, priprema ponuda i zakazivanje razgovora.

2.title: Online prodavnice
2.description: Preporuke proizvoda, pomoć pri pretrazi, odgovori o isporuci i podrška nakon kupovine.

3.title: Poljoprivreda i savetovanje
3.description: Pretraga konkursa, usmeravanje korisnika, baze znanja i pomoć pri izboru relevantnih usluga.

4.title: Administracija i poslovni sistemi
4.description: Obrada dokumenata, kategorizacija zahteva, sažeci i automatizovano prosleđivanje zadataka.

process.label: Način rada
process.title: Kako uvodimo AI u poslovni proces
process.description: Kvalitetno AI rešenje počinje analizom procesa, a ne izborom alata.

1.title: Analiza procesa
1.description: Utvrđujemo gde nastaje najviše ponavljajućeg rada, čekanja ili nepotrebnog ručnog unosa.

2.title: Procena podataka i rizika
2.description: Proveravamo koje podatke sistem koristi, kako se čuvaju i gde je neophodna kontrola zaposlenog.

3.title: Pilot rešenje
3.description: Pravimo ograničenu verziju, testiramo odgovore i proveravamo da li donosi merljivu korist.

4.title: Uvođenje i unapređivanje
4.description: Postepeno širimo funkcionalnosti, pratimo rezultate i prilagođavamo sistem realnom radu.

control.label: Odgovorna primena
control.title: AI pomaže zaposlenima — ne ukida ljudsku kontrolu
control.description: Važne poslovne, finansijske, pravne i stručne odluke ne treba prepustiti sistemu bez provere. Zato jasno definišemo šta AI može da uradi samostalno, a kada uključuje odgovornu osobu.

items.1: Korisnik treba da zna kada komunicira sa AI sistemom
items.2: Osetljivi podaci se koriste samo kada za to postoji opravdana potreba
items.3: Kritični odgovori i odluke zahtevaju proveru odgovorne osobe
items.4: Sistem se prati, testira i unapređuje na osnovu stvarnih rezultata

cta.title: Imate proces koji oduzima previše vremena?
cta.description: Opišite nam kako trenutno radite, gde nastaju zastoji i šta biste želeli da ubrzate. Procenićemo da li AI i automatizacija mogu da donesu stvarnu korist.
